steps.title: Original price equation
steps.content: The variables represent original prices: $l$ is the lamp's original price and $c$ is the clock's original price. Since the two original prices total \$80, the first equation is $$l + c = 80$$

steps.title: Sale price equation
steps.content:
After discounts, the customer pays:
  • Lamp (20% discount): customer pays 100% - 20% = 80% of original = $0.80l$
  • Clock (40% discount): customer pays 100% - 40% = 60% of original = $0.60c$
Combined sale price is \$56: $$0.80l + 0.60c = 56$$
That confirms C is correct.
